# Phase 0 Research: Deck UX Hardening

All decisions below resolve the open questions from the spec/plan. No `NEEDS CLARIFICATION` remain.

## D1 — P4 canonical-source direction (resolves spec Assumption, medium confidence)

**Decision**: `index.html` is the single source of truth; `slides/NN-*.html` are generated by `scripts/split-slides.mjs`. No consolidation of two hand-maintained sources is needed.

**Rationale**: `scripts/split-slides.mjs` already extracts each `<section class="slide">` from `index.html` and writes standalone per-slide files. Regenerating produced a zero-diff `git status` against the committed `slides/*.html`, confirming they are already derivative and in sync. The real gap is FR-017 (a check that *fails on divergence*): the generator has no `--check` mode and is not wired into `npm run verify`, so drift could be introduced silently.

**Scope reduction**: P4 shrinks from "consolidate two sources" to "add drift detection." Add `--check` to `split-slides.mjs` (regenerate in memory, compare to on-disk files, exit non-zero on any mismatch) and a `check:slides` npm script inside `verify`.

**Alternatives considered**:
- *Invert direction (per-slide files canonical, assemble index.html)*: rejected — `index.html` is the authored showcase with narrative ordering (e.g., reference placed after closing) and shared `<head>`; inverting adds an assembler with no benefit.
- *Templating engine / partials*: rejected — introduces a build dependency, violating the no-runtime-build, static-file constraint.

## D2 — Non-color chart series encoding (resolves FR-004, spec Assumption)

**Decision**: Add **direct labels** as the primary non-color cue on multi-series charts, keeping the DS 7-category palette for color. Where direct labels would crowd (e.g., 100% stacked bars, donut), add an in-SVG value/segment label. Series identity must be recoverable in grayscale.

**Rationale**: Cleveland–McGill and `C-preattentive` favor direct labeling over legends; direct labels also satisfy WCAG 1.4.1 (use of color) without inventing pattern fills that fight the DS palette and data-ink discipline (`C-data-ink`). Labels are real DOM/SVG `<text>` (consistent with `convertibility.spec.mjs` expecting real text, not pseudo-elements).

**Applies to**: Appendix D multi-series bar (企画/開発/運用), Appendix E multi-line, 100% stacked bar, donut, scatter. The single-series chart (06) already labels bars ("内製 4h" / "テンプレ 1h") — no change needed.

**Alternatives considered**:
- *SVG pattern fills (hatching)*: rejected as default — adds visual noise, harder to keep AA, and duplicates what a label conveys. Kept as a fallback only where labels cannot fit.
- *Redundant legend only*: rejected — a color-keyed legend still fails color-blind users; direct labels remove the color dependency at the mark.

## D3 — Present mode activation & scaling (resolves FR-009–014, spec Assumption)

**Decision**: Present mode is **opt-in**, toggled by a toolbar control and keyboard (`P`), reflected on a root attribute (e.g. `data-mode="present"`), defaulting to `review`. Scaling uses a single CSS `transform: scale()` on the active slide computed from `viewport / 1280×720`, centered, aspect-ratio preserved. Navigation: `→`/`Space`/`PageDown` next, `←`/`PageUp` prev, bounded (no wrap past first/last); `F` toggles Fullscreen API; `Esc`/`P` exits present mode. Non-active slides get `inert`/`hidden` so they are not focusable or announced (FR-014). All transitions gated behind `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)`.

**Rationale**: An opt-in root-attribute mode leaves the default review rendering byte-identical at native size, so existing `assertNoOverflow`, screenshot, axe, and print tests remain green (they never toggle the mode). `transform: scale()` fits any viewport without reflowing the fixed 1280×720 canvas (layout math stays constant → no new overflow). At exactly 1280×720 the scale factor is 1 → visually identical. The Fullscreen API and `prefers-reduced-motion` are standard, dependency-free.

**Print/review safety**: `@media print` forces all slides visible (existing rule) and ignores `data-mode`; present.js is only referenced by `index.html`, not by generated per-slide files (keeps `split-slides` head template unchanged and per-slide a11y tests stable).

**Key-conflict check**: `annotations.js` binds `a`; `frame.js` binds nothing. Present mode uses `p`, `f`, arrows, space, PageUp/Down, Esc — no collision. Both skip when focus is in INPUT/TEXTAREA/SELECT.

**Alternatives considered**:
- *CSS `zoom`*: rejected — non-standard, inconsistent box metrics, poor print interaction.
- *Default-on present mode*: rejected — breaks native-size test assumptions and the scroll-review affordance.
- *Third-party framework (reveal.js, etc.)*: rejected — heavy dependency, conflicts with token/DS discipline and the static no-build constraint.

## D4 — Focus indicator styling (resolves FR-001)

**Decision**: Global `:focus-visible` outline using existing tokens: `outline: var(--space-1)/2 solid var(--accent)` equivalent expressed via tokens plus `outline-offset`, with a non-accent fallback on accent-colored surfaces (title/closing) to preserve ≥3:1 non-text contrast. Remove default `:focus` outline suppression only where a visible `:focus-visible` replaces it.

**Rationale**: `:focus-visible` shows the ring for keyboard users without affecting mouse users; token-driven keeps `lint:tokens` green. Accent (#0031d8) on white gives ample contrast; on the accent background slides the ring switches to `--text-on-accent` (white) for the 3:1 requirement.

**Alternatives considered**:
- *`:focus` (not `:focus-visible`)*: rejected — shows rings on mouse click, noisier; `:focus-visible` is broadly supported in target browsers.
- *box-shadow ring*: viable but `outline` avoids layout/paint edge cases and never triggers overflow; chosen for that reason.

## D5 — Heading & landmark structure (resolves FR-002, FR-003)

**Decision**: Title slide heading becomes `<h1 class="title">` (was `<p>`); every other slide keeps `<h2 class="slide__heading">`. Section-label lines (`.section-no`, `SECTION 01`) stay non-heading text. Wrap `.deck` in `<main>`. Each `<section class="slide">` gets `aria-roledescription="スライド"` and an `aria-label` naming the slide and position; page position already computed by `frame.js` can supply "N / total".

**Rationale**: Exactly one `<h1>` yields a valid outline; `<main>` provides the required landmark; `aria-roledescription` + `aria-label` give AT users slide identity/position (FR-003) without visual change. Changing the title element from `p` to `h1` does not affect `.title` selectors (class preserved) so `convertibility.spec.mjs` and layout CSS remain valid.

**Alternatives considered**:
- *`role="region"` per slide*: rejected — too many landmarks clutter AT navigation; `aria-roledescription` on a section is lighter and semantically accurate.
- *Multiple h1 (one per slide)*: rejected — violates single-outline expectation and SC-003.
